steps.title: Read the center and radius
steps.content:
Circle $M$ has equation
$$(x-5)^2+(y-8)^2=9$$
So its center is $(5,8)$ and its radius is
$$\sqrt{9}=3$$

steps.title: Double the radius
steps.content:
Circle $P$ has the same center, but its radius is twice as large.
$$2\cdot3=6$$
In a circle equation, the right side is $r^2$, so the new right side is
$$6^2=36$$
The answer is B.
